description: Splunk AI assistant metadata: indexes, sourcetypes, fields, and lookups for organization environment.

indexes.snow: ServiceNow incidents, changes, and CMDB data.
indexes.pan_logs: Palo Alto firewall and threat logs.
indexes.network: Network device and circuit telemetry, including unit_id and circuit fields.
indexes.wineventlog: Windows Security and System EventLog data.
indexes.linux_auth: Linux authentication and system auth logs.
indexes.api: API gateway or backend HTTP access logs.
indexes.os: Operating system metrics and host-level telemetry.

fields.unit_id: Identifier for a business unit, site, or logical network grouping.
fields.circuit: Identifier for a physical or logical network circuit/link.
fields.network: CIDR or network key used to join with the infoblox_networks_lite lookup.
fields.u_business_unit: ServiceNow field mapping incidents to a business unit.
fields.u_business_service: ServiceNow field representing the impacted business service.

unit_id_list.description: Lookup mapping unit_id and circuit to human-readable names and attributes.

infoblox_networks_lite.description: Lookup mapping network CIDRs to organization, unit_id, circuit, and region metadata.

rules.no_index_star: Never use index=* or recommend index=* in SPL. Always ask which index or indexes are relevant.
rules.prefer_tstats: Prefer | tstats over raw searches whenever using indexed fields or accelerated data models.
rules.use_term_prefix: Use TERM() and PREFIX() to leverage index-time tokenization for faster searches.
rules.prefer_cim: Prefer CIM-compliant fields and datamodel searches (datamodel=Authentication, datamodel=Web, etc.) when available.
rules.ask_for_index: If the user doesn’t specify an index and it isn’t obvious from context, ask which index or dataset they mean.
